== recent hyper inflation of the currency valve ==

Hello Wikipedians, recently (January 6, 2026) there was a hyper inflation rate of the Iranian Rial, I think we should put the mark the was on the article “Venezuela” had a day or two ago because due to it dropping from 30,000 to 40,000 to over a million per US dollar, and we are not sure if it’s going to drop or not. [[User:Qhiq|Qhiq]] ([[User talk:Qhiq|talk]]) 22:24, 12 January 2026 (UTC)

== CIA market manipulation ==

The source does not match the text, so I've boldly removed it for the time being. It relies on two sources. [https://web.archive.org/web/20070909142455/http://www.msnbc.msn.com/id/18881815/site/newsweek/] [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304205908/http://www.payvand.com/news/12/oct/1190.html]

First source made no mention of authorizing market manipulation. It did mention 'authorizing the CIA to use covert methods to harass'. However, making the jump from that to currency market manipulation seemed a bit too far. While it did mention "According to the ABC News investigative unit... CIA plan reportedly includes... manipulation of Iran's currency", it's not by Newsweek. And it didn't state that was the plan authorized.

Second source made no mention of CIA at all. It just mentioned forex market manipulator(s) arrested. There's no mention of foreign connection. 

If anyone have better sources, please leave it here. [[User:Anothercat613|Anothercat613]] ([[User talk:Anothercat613|talk]]) 16:37, 24 January 2026 (UTC)
